alerts.title: Trump announces framework for Iran negotiations; halts threatened strikes
alerts.summary: President Trump declared June 11 that he will no longer conduct bombing operations against Iran tonight, citing approval of a negotiation framework by both sides (per ME Spectator Telegram, ~42K views). Statement represents sharp reversal from earlier threats to seize Kharg Island and strike oil infrastructure, signaling possible de-escalation or tactical pause after two days of kinetic exchanges. High-confidence source: multiple Telegram channels and mainstream outlets (PBS, AP, Axios) confirm announcement.

alerts.title: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz following continued U.S.-Iran strike exchange
alerts.summary: Iran has closed the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing U.S. bombardment campaigns on June 11 (per Al Jazeera incident report). Action represents escalatory response to sustained U.S. kinetic operations and signals Tehran's willingness to weaponize critical global chokepoint. Closure threatens ~30% of world seaborne oil trade and correlates with Brent crude holding at $89.09/barrel and Iranian rial free-market rate at 1,375,155 IRR/USD.

alerts.title: Iran uranium enrichment remains at 60% U-235; 1-2 week breakout window intact amid kinetic war
alerts.summary: IAEA continues to confirm Iran maintains 60% uranium enrichment with estimated 1-2 week nuclear breakout timeline despite active U.S.-Iran military operations (per global_metrics, nuclear headline sources). IAEA board passed resolution demanding Iran provide full stockpile reporting transparency. Concurrent nuclear advancement and kinetic escalation represent compounding strategic risk.

alerts.title: U.S. sentiment toward Iran conflict turns sharply negative; Trump dominates discourse
alerts.summary: U.S. sentiment analysis shows 49% negative vs. 44% positive polarization on June 11, with 'Trump' (88 instances), 'Iran' (18), 'Clayton' (17), and 'war' (9) dominating word cloud. Negative sentiment spike reflects second straight day of U.S. strike operations and public concern over nuclear/escalation risk. Sentiment shift indicates domestic political pressure on Trump administration to justify continued military action.

alerts.title: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon kill 17; proxy escalation persists amid ceasefire
alerts.summary: Israeli military conducted strikes on southern Lebanon June 11, resulting in 17 reported fatalities (per BBC incident report, lat 33.52°N, lon 36.08°E). Attack occurred during fragile Israel-Iran ceasefire period and signals ongoing proxy-axis tensions despite Trump's negotiation framework announcement. Demonstrates that regional de-escalation remains incomplete across all conflict vectors.

analysis_note: The theater remains in acute flux: Trump's negotiation framework announcement and strike halt create opening for possible U.S.-Iran talks, but Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz, maintained 60% enrichment, and Israeli-Hezbollah kinetic activity indicate preparedness for continued escalation if diplomacy fails. Threat level remains at 9/10 (critical).
